mission: Map plugin lifecycle terrain in packages/opencode/src/plugin/ and packages/plugin/
scope: read-only cartography — 8 surfaces, trust boundaries, parallel data structures, missing typed errors

files.role: Central orchestrator: Plugin Service (Effect Layer), trigger dispatch, init lifecycle, applyPlugin, capability registration, crash/quarantine persistence
External plugin pipeline: plan → resolve (3-stage) → load (dynamic import) → loadExternal with retry logic
Resolution utilities: npm-package-arg parsing, entrypoint detection, compatibility checks, readPluginId, readV1Plugin, resolvePluginId
CLI install: installPlugin, readPluginManifest, patchPluginConfig (patches opencode.json)
14 CapabilityId values, CapabilityManifest, TrustLevel, PluginSecurityState
Registry class wrapping Ref<Map<string, PluginSecurityState>> per pluginId
checkCapability() — quarantined→deny, built-in→allow, else manifest check
HOOK_CAPABILITY_MAP (14 mappings) and ALWAYS_ALLOWED_HOOKS (4 bypass hooks)
shouldDispatch() — 4-step gate per hook dispatch including network gate
Barrel re-export
Codex OAuth auth plugin (static import, trustLevel=built-in)
OpenAI Responses WebSocket transport
WebSocket connection pool with auth header injection
GitHub Copilot OAuth auth plugin
Copilot model catalog fetch
xAI device-code OAuth auth plugin
DigitalOcean implicit OAuth auth plugin
Azure token passthrough auth plugin (stub)
Cloudflare AI Gateway + Workers auth plugins (stubs)
Public API types: Plugin, PluginModule, PluginInput, Hooks (21 hook members), AuthHook, ProviderHook, Config, WorkspaceAdapter
ErrorCode enum (10 values), makeError(), parseToolError(), isSuccess(), isError()
ToolDefinition, tool() factory, ToolContext
DesktopPlugin, DesktopPluginModule, PluginTransport, DesktopPluginApi
TuiPlugin, TuiPluginModule, TuiPluginApi, route/slot definitions
BunShell interface — the $ shell passed to plugins
Example plugin stub
Example workspace adapter

config_files.role: Loads/merges opencode.json: resolveLoadedPlugins (L117-123), mergePluginOrigins (L537-567), auto-discovery hook (L678), plugin_origins derived state
Schema: Spec, Origin, Scope, load() for Glob.scan auto-discovery, resolvePluginSpec(), deduplicatePluginOrigins()

unit: test/plugin/ (14 files: capability-proxy-bisect, loader-shared, shared, install, install-concurrency, trigger, workspace-adapter, xai, codex, openai-ws, openai-rollout, cloudflare, github-copilot-models, auth-override)
integration: test/cli/tui/ (7 files: plugin-loader, plugin-toggle, plugin-install, plugin-add, plugin-loader-entrypoint, plugin-loader-pure, plugin-lifecycle)
fixture: test/fixture/ (4 files: plugin, tui-plugin, agent-plugin, agent-plugin.constants)

discovery_channels.how: Static TypeScript imports at module parse time — internalPlugins() returns hardcoded array of 9 auth plugins
opencode.json 'plugin' array field. resolveLoadedPlugins() normalizes path specs relative to config file. Each item is string spec or [spec, Options]
ConfigPlugin.load() uses Glob.scan('{plugin,plugins}/*.{ts,js}') inside .opencode directory. Appended as file:// URLs with scope=local

stages.name: resolve:install
stages.what: resolvePluginTarget() — npm install or path resolve

stages.name: resolve:entry
stages.what: createPluginEntry() — inspect package.json exports or directory index

stages.name: resolve:compat
stages.what: checkPluginCompatibility() — semver check for npm plugins, file plugins skip

stages.name: apply
stages.what: readV1Plugin() or getLegacyPlugins() → calls plugin.server(input, options) → resolves pluginId

registries.structure: State = { hooks: Hooks[], registrations: PluginRegistration[], capabilityRegistry, dispatchGuard } — in-memory only
Registry class wrapping Ref<Map<string, PluginSecurityState>> — tracks trustLevel, manifest, crashCount, quarantined per pluginId

trust_boundary_issues.issue: Dynamic import() executes arbitrary module code in-process before any capability check — top-level side effects run immediately
Auto-discovery loads any .ts/.js file dropped into .opencode/plugin(s)/ without explicit user opt-in
Bun caches failed dynamic imports — retry after dependency install only works for pre-import failures (isRetryableResolveError), not import-time failures

surface_2_config_resolution.merge_order: global → project → auto-discovered (last-wins deduplication by plugin identity)
surface_2_config_resolution.dedup_key: npm package name OR file:// URL (config/plugin.ts:69-84)

schema.Options: Record<string, unknown> — completely unstructured, no validation

surface_2_config_resolution.plugin_origins_note: Derived state (config.ts:312-314), not persisted — attached during config merge so callers know which file won and where follow-up writes should go

trust_boundary_issues.issue: Plugin Options is Record<string, unknown> — completely unstructured JSON, no validation, no secrets detection at config boundary

built_in.usage: Used for all 9 built-in auth plugins

verified.description: DEFINED BUT NEVER USED — zero code paths assign this trust level

surface_3_capability_grant_model.declaration: External plugins get FALLBACK_MANIFEST = { capabilities: ['tool.register'] } (enforcer.ts:9). No plugin-provided manifest is read at runtime — manifest comes from config layer, not plugin code.

runtime_check.model: per-operation — re-validated on every trigger() call, not one-time gate

trust_boundary_issues.issue: 'verified' trust level is defined in types but has zero code paths that assign it — no middle trust tier, binary built-in vs external only
Pervasive 'as any' casts on capability strings (index.ts:332,437,443,476) bypass the CapabilityId union type entirely
If an npm dependency of a built-in plugin is compromised via supply-chain, all capabilities are open because trustLevel=built-in

registration_flow.action: readV1Plugin(mod) checks for {id, server} export shape
Fallback: getLegacyPlugins(mod) scans all exports for callable PluginInstance
Registers security state (trustLevel, manifest) in capabilityRegistry
Calls config hook immediately if present

dispatch_flow.action: trigger(name, input, output) iterates s.hooks array serially
If ALWAYS_ALLOWED_HOOKS → call fn directly, no capability check
dispatchGuard.shouldDispatch(name, pluginId): ALWAYS_ALLOWED→true, unknown→false, check capability, check network gate
Call fn(input, output) via Effect.tryPromise — output object SHARED (mutated in-place)
On error: log.error, catch, continue to next plugin

surface_4_hook_registration_and_invocati.execution_model: Serial, async. Each plugin's hook runs to completion before next. Output is shared mutable reference — all plugins write to same object.
surface_4_hook_registration_and_invocati.cleanup: dispose hook run via Effect.addFinalizer on scope close. No per-hook unsubscribe — removal all-or-nothing via scope. Adapter registrations (registerAdapter) NOT cleaned up during dispose.

trust_boundary_issues.issue: ALWAYS_ALLOWED_HOOKS bypass ALL capability checks — external plugins receive full config, auth credentials, and provider secrets with zero capability gate
No timeout/abort on hook handler execution — a hung handler blocks the entire serial dispatch loop indefinitely
Output object is shared mutable reference — one plugin can corrupt output for downstream consumers or other plugins in the serial chain
init() lifecycle runs before hook-specific capability gating — plugins can perform side effects during init() without capability checks

per_hook_isolation.mechanism: Effect.tryPromise with catch — error logged, NOT re-thrown, loop continues to next plugin

crash_quarantine.mechanics: persistCrash() increments crashCount → if >=3 sets quarantined=true on capability registry → ALL capabilities denied (enforcer.ts:26)
crash_quarantine.reset_path: persistSuccess() resets crashCount to 0 — BUT only updates PluginHealthStore, NOT capability registry's crashCount

trust_boundary_issues.issue: Crash count resets to 0 on any successful hook invocation — attacker can oscillate crash→succeed→crash to avoid quarantine
Hook failures only logged, not surfaced via bus events — no pub/sub for plugin error notifications

two_disconnected_mechanisms.description: Reports single 'plugin' component with status=Healthy. Exposed via GET /global/health. Does NOT surface per-plugin crash/quarantine state.

JSON file at {globalDataDir}/plugin-health.json. Tracks per pluginId: crashCount, quarantined, lastCrashed. NOT exposed via any HTTP API. Only accessible via Plugin.Service.getCrashStatus() (Effect-only).

error_reporting.path: Hook dispatch errors: log.error('plugin hook error', {pluginId, hook, error})
Config hook errors: log.error('plugin config hook failed')

surface_6_diagnostics.issue: Global health endpoint reports aggregate 'plugin' status=healthy unconditionally — cannot surface per-plugin degradation even when individual plugins are crashed/quarantined

resolution_chain.rule: File plugins MUST export 'id' field — throws TypeError if missing
npm plugins use exported 'id' if present, else npm package name from package.json
Legacy/unnamed plugins fall back to module's specifier string

surface_7_identity_model.type: string — no structured PluginId type exists
surface_7_identity_model.namespace_handling: None. Two npm plugins with same package name collide. Dedup relies on string equality of npm package name or file:// URL. No namespace prefix or organizational hierarchy.
surface_7_identity_model.logging: pluginId can be undefined in error paths → logged as 'unknown' making forensic diagnosis impossible

trust_boundary_issues.issue: No structured PluginId type — identity is plain string with no validation, no namespace, no collision detection beyond config dedup

disposal_mechanism.action: Effect.addFinalizer → for each hooks object, call dispose?.()
Wrapped in try/catch — failures logged, NOT propagated, never prevent shutdown
Effect scope closure handles fiber interruption for event bus subscriptions (forkScoped)

limitations: No explicit unloadPlugin/removePlugin/disablePlugin API — plugins live for the lifetime of their Effect scope
registerAdapter() calls during plugin init NOT cleaned up during dispose — workspace adapters persist beyond plugin lifecycle
No graceful vs forced distinction — dispose always runs, always caught, no timeout, no kill mechanism
dispose is an ALWAYS_ALLOWED_HOOK — a quarantined plugin's dispose still executes, giving one last execution opportunity

parallel_data_structures.desync: persistSuccess() (index.ts:413) resets crashCount in health store but NOT in capability registry — capability registry retains stale crashCount after successful load
Global health endpoint cannot surface per-plugin degradation — aggregate status always 'healthy' even when individual plugins are crashed/quarantined

missing_typed_errors.what_missing: No PluginDisposeError — caught as unknown, logged generically, caller cannot distinguish dispose failures from other lifecycle errors
No PluginConfigError with pluginId and hook context — raw err passed to log.error
Two separate try/catch blocks log 'plugin hook error' with pluginId='unknown' (line ~433) vs pluginId variable (line ~448) — no error taxonomy
All failures wrapped in NamedError.Unknown — no PluginInstallError, PluginCompatibilityError, PluginLoadError for stage discrimination
All errors become NamedError.Unknown — no PluginError with stage-specific subclasses

known_failure_classes.description: Hook dispatch silently skips (only log.warn) when capability check fails — caller has no way to know a hook was denied
import() in loader.ts:138 evaluates the entire module — malicious top-level code runs before any capability gating
Bun caches failed dynamic imports — retry after wait() only works for pre-import failures (isRetryableResolveError), not import-time failures like build errors or syntax errors
Plugin can't selectively unregister individual hooks — removal is all-or-nothing via scope closure
File plugins (.opencode/plugin/*.ts) skip checkPluginCompatibility — only npm plugins are version-gated
